codeQualityRules.documentation: 80-100 line block comment at top of each file. No inline comments. Doc comments (///) for public API only.
codeQualityRules.errorHandling: Use eyre exclusively. Every error must include context (operation, resource, reason). No custom error enums.
codeQualityRules.followPatterns: Study existing code in src/ directories. Follow established patterns. Reference Go implementation for logic.
codeQualityRules.naming: Follow Rust naming conventions. snake_case for functions/variables, PascalCase for types.
codeQualityRules.noPartialWork: Each task must be 100% complete before marking done=true
codeQualityRules.noPlaceholders: NEVER write TODO comments or placeholder implementations. Complete each task fully.
codeQualityRules.testsMustPass: All tests must pass before committing. Run 'cargo test' to verify
codeQualityRules.zeroAllocation: CRUD operations must not allocate. Use arenas for query-scoped allocations.
codeQualityRules.zeroCopy: Return &[u8] slices from mmap. Never clone page data.

examples: feat(storage): implement SIEVE cache eviction algorithm
feat(btree): add slot array with prefix hints for fast lookup
test(hnsw): add benchmarks for SQ8 quantized distance
fix(mvcc): correct version chain visibility check
scopes: storage - Storage engine (pager, mmap, cache, wal)

concurrency.avoidConflicts: Before starting, check 'git worktree list' for active feature worktrees
concurrency.cleanup: After merging a feature, remove the worktree: 'git worktree remove ./tur-feature-<name>'
concurrency.mergeStrategy: Each feature worktree is isolated. After completion, merge from the worktree or create PR
concurrency.multipleAgents: Multiple agents can work on DIFFERENT features simultaneously using git worktrees

beforeStarting: Read this entire workflow section carefully
Check active worktrees: run 'git worktree list' to see active feature worktrees
Pick ONE feature where isCompleted=false AND no worktree exists for it
implementationCycle: STEP 1: Create feature worktree with dedicated directory
STEP 2: For EACH task in the feature.tasks array (in order):
→ Identify what files need to be created/modified
→ Check Go implementation in ../pkg/ for logic reference
→ Create or update test module in tests/ directory
→ Write specific test case for this task
→ VERIFY test FAILS (if it passes, test is wrong or task already done)
→ Write MINIMAL code to make the test pass
→ Ensure zero-copy and zero-allocation where required
→ Focus on making THIS test pass
→ Clean up code while keeping tests green
→ Find this exact task in the feature
REPEAT 2a-2f for ALL tasks in the feature
→ Commit: git commit -m 'feat(<scope>): complete feature implementation'
→ Report any blockers or dependencies discovered

workflow.objective: Achieve CONCRETE, WORKING implementation of TurDB Rust features. Zero-copy, zero allocation, 16KB pages, 1MB RAM minimum.

referenceResources.testExamples: Look at Rust crate tests for testing patterns

testingGuidelines.coverage: Aim for >80% code coverage per module
testingGuidelines.location: Tests in #[cfg(test)] mod tests { } at end of file, or tests/ directory for integration

troubleshooting.conflictingWorktree: Feature worktree already exists? Pick a different feature.
troubleshooting.lifetimeErrors: Borrow checker issues? Review CLAUDE.md zero-copy patterns. Consider arena allocation.
troubleshooting.missingDependencies: Task depends on another incomplete feature? Note as blocker. Implement dependency first.
troubleshooting.testWontFail: Test passes immediately? Either task is already done, or test isn't testing the right thing.
troubleshooting.testWontPass: Can't make test pass? Break task into smaller steps. Study Go implementation.
troubleshooting.worktreeCleanup: When done: 'git worktree remove <path>' to clean up

Implement page() returning &[u8] slice (zero-copy)

Implement grow() to extend file and remap
Add sync() for fsync to disk
Handle mmap remap on grow (update existing page references)

Implement allocate() to get free page
Implement release() to return page to freelist
Implement sync() to persist freelist to disk
Handle trunk page overflow (create new trunk)

Implement create_schema() to create schema directory

Implement table_index() returning index MmapStorage

features.title: SQL - Lexer
features.description: Zero-copy SQL tokenizer supporting full SQL syntax including DDL, DML, subqueries, CTEs, batch operations, explain/analyze, stored procedures, functions, and all standard SQL operators

tasks.task: Define Token<'a> enum with borrowed strings for identifiers and literals
Define Keyword enum covering DDL, DML, query clauses, joins, set operations, subquery, CTE, transactions, constraints, indexes, types, procedural, explain, schema objects
Define Lexer<'a> with input slice, position, line, column for error reporting
Implement next_token() returning Token<'a> with zero-copy string slices
Implement keyword matching via phf perfect hash map for O(1) lookup
Handle single-quoted string literals with '' escape for embedded quotes
Handle double-quoted identifiers for case-sensitive/reserved word columns
Handle backtick-quoted identifiers for reserved words
Handle dollar-quoted strings ($$body$$, $tag$body$tag$) for procedure bodies
Handle numeric literals: integers, floats, scientific notation, hex, binary, octal
Handle identifiers: unquoted (starts with letter/_), quoted, backtick-quoted
Handle operators: comparison, arithmetic, bitwise, string concat, JSON, array
Handle punctuation: parentheses, brackets, braces, comma, semicolon, colon, double-colon, dot
Handle comments: single-line (-- comment), multi-line (/* */), nested multi-line
Handle parameter placeholders: positional ($1, $2), named (:name, @name), anonymous (?)
Implement peek() and peek_nth() for parser lookahead without consuming
Implement span() returning byte range for error messages
Add comprehensive tests for all token types and edge cases
